chemicals.name: Green tomatoes belong to the nightshade (Solanaceae) plant family and contain potent defensive compounds that are highly toxic to cats

chemicals.concentration_notes: or potency: The concentration of these toxic alkaloids is dangerously high in unripe green tomatoes, as well as in the leaves, stems, and vines of the tomato plant1.... Cats are extraordinarily sensitive to these plant toxins compared to dogs because they lack specific hepatic detoxification pathways (glucuronidation); this slows down their ability to process the toxins, meaning even very small am
chemicals.description: in cats: These glycoalkaloid neurotoxins harm cats through three primary mechanisms: Gastrointestinal Irritation (Cytotoxicity): The toxins interact directly with the phospholipids of the cell membranes, increasing cell permeability and causing cell destruction5. Intestinal epithelial cells are particularly vulnerable, resulting in severe mucosal irritation, inflammation, and violent lower gastroi
chemicals.name: Glycoalkaloids (Solanine and Tomatine)

description: Ripe red tomatoes (Solanum lycopersicum) are not significantly toxic to cats, but green (unripe) tomatoes contain solanine and tomatine — glycoalkaloids found throughout the nightshade family. These compounds decrease as the fruit ripens, which is why ripe tomatoes are generally considered safe in small amounts while all green parts of the tomato plant remain hazardous.
emergencyNote: 1. Emergency Stabilization (Neurological, Respiratory, and Cardiovascular Support)
name: Green tomatoes
safetyNotes: Avoid: green (unripe) tomatoes and all green parts of the plant — leaves, stems, and vines contain solanine and tomatine
Ripe red tomato flesh: safe in very small amounts but offers no nutritional benefit to cats
Symptoms if green parts ingested: drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, weakness; severe exposure may cause bradycardia or CNS depression
Never feed seasoned tomato products — garlic, onion, and salt in sauces or ketchup are toxic to cats
If your cat chews tomato plant leaves or eats a green tomato, contact your vet

symptoms.name: Green tomatoes, as well as the leaves and stems of the tomato plant
Severe Gastrointestinal Upset
symptoms.notes: This is typically the first sign of nightshade poisoning. Solanine is a potent cytotoxin that interacts with the phospholipids in cell membranes, causing direct cell destruction and violent irritation and inflammation of the intestinal epithelial cells1.... A tense abdominal wall and pain upon being
symptoms.onset: Usually within 2 to 24 hours of ingestion,

symptoms.name: Hypersalivation
symptoms.notes: While it can be a reaction to the severe mucosal irritation in the mouth and throat, drooling is primarily driven by the neurotoxic properties of the glycoalkaloids, which overstimulate the parasympathetic nervous system

symptoms.name: Neurological Deficits and Paralysis
symptoms.notes: Solanine acts as a neurotoxin by inhibiting acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme responsible for breaking down the neurotransmitter acetylcholine. This leads to massive acetylcholine accumulation at the synaptic cleft and parasympathetic overstimulation1.... Additionally, the toxins disrupt mitochondrial

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular Disturbances
symptoms.notes: The severe parasympathetic overstimulation caused by the accumulation of acetylcholine forces the heart rate to slow dangerously. In critical cases, this progresses to complete circulatory failure and cardiovascular collapse, which can be fatal

symptoms.name: Ocular Changes
symptoms.notes: Dilated pupils are another direct clinical manifestation of the neurotoxicity and autonomic nervous system overstimulation caused by the glycoalkaloids

symptoms.name: Hemolysis and Acute Kidney Injury
symptoms.notes: In highly severe cases, solanine toxicity causes the cat's red blood cells to be destroyed (hemolysis)67. The release of free hemoglobin into the bloodstream from the ruptured cells is highly nephrotoxic (toxic to the kidneys) and can secondarily destroy the kidneys, leading to acute renal failure
symptoms.onset: Progressive; typically occurs in the later stages of a severe poisoning episode

treatments.description: of the procedure: If the cat presents with advanced symptoms, the veterinary team will immediately administer oxygen supplementation or provide mechanical ventilation to address respiratory depression12. Seizures and convulsions are controlled using anticonvulsant medications such as benzodiazepines or barbiturates13. In critical cases presenting with severe heart rhythm abnormalities or low blood
treatments.name: 1. Emergency Stabilization (Neurological, Respiratory, and Cardiovascular Support)
treatments.notes: This is the absolute highest priority if the cat arrives already exhibiting severe neurological or cardiac signs, because the primary cause of death in fatal solanine poisoning is respiratory paralysis and circulatory failure

treatments.description: of the procedure: If the green tomatoes were ingested very recently (within 1 to 2 hours) and the cat is not yet showing severe symptoms, the veterinarian will administer a specific medication to empty the stomach37. Following emesis, activated charcoal is administered orally (and potentially repeated at 4- to 6-hour intervals) to bind any unabsorbed glycoalkaloids in the gastrointestinal tract an
treatments.name: Early Gastrointestinal Decontamination
treatments.notes: To induce vomiting safely in cats, veterinarians use specific α 2 ​ -adrenergic agonists, such as dexmedetomidine or xylazine78. If the cat is already heavily sedated or showing advanced neurological symptoms, inducing vomiting is unsafe; in these cases, the veterinarian will place the cat under ane

treatments.description: of the procedure: The veterinarian administers the medication atropine to help counteract the specific neurotoxic effects of the glycoalkaloids19. Any situation-specific
treatments.name: Parasympathetic Tone Management
treatments.notes: While there is no true, direct antidote for solanine or tomatine poisoning, these toxins inhibit the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, leading to an overwhelming accumulation of acetylcholine and severe parasympathetic overstimulation5.... Atropine is used specifically to help compensate for and block th

treatments.description: of the procedure: The cat is hospitalized and placed on continuous IV fluids to correct the severe dehydration caused by violent vomiting and diarrhea, and to stimulate diuresis (urine production) to flush the toxins from the body13. The veterinary team will closely monitor the cat's blood counts, vital parameters, and kidney function14. Any situation-specific
treatments.name: Intravenous (IV) Fluid Diuresis and Renal Protection
treatments.notes: In severe nightshade poisonings, the solanine toxins can destroy the cat's red blood cells (hemolysis)5. The free hemoglobin released from ruptured cells is highly toxic to the kidneys and can cause acute renal failure511. If hemolysis is detected, the veterinarian must significantly increase the IV
